_sources.discrepancies: DC uses the Board of Elections (DCBOE), not a Secretary of State. NCSL classifies DC as 'no document required' for ID. DC is a universal vote-by-mail jurisdiction — all registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot. DC also has automatic voter registration via DMV. DC's 2026 primary is typically in late June; TODO verify exact 2026 date. DC voters elect non-voting delegate to Congress (Eleanor Holmes Norton), DC Council, Mayor, and other local offices. DC has same-day registration. No statewide runoff rule.

earlyVoting.notes: DC offers early in-person voting at vote centers across the District. All active registered voters also receive mail ballots automatically. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
DC offers early in-person voting at vote centers. All active registered voters receive mail ballots automatically. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
DC is a universal vote-by-mail jurisdiction. All active registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot. Early in-person voting also available at vote centers. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: DC does not prohibit phones in polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot or interfere with other voters. Electioneering is prohibited within 50 feet of a polling place.
additionalRules: DC is a universal vote-by-mail jurisdiction: all active registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot before each election.
Automatic voter registration via DC DMV when applying for or renewing a driver's license or ID.
Same-day voter registration available at polling places and vote centers.
DC voters elect a non-voting Delegate to the U.S. House of Representatives and local officials (Mayor, DC Council, etc.).
No senators or voting representatives in Congress — DC residents have limited federal representation.

eligibility: All active registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot — no application needed
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Not required; ballots automatically mailed to all active registered voters
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by DCBOE by 8 p.m. on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within 7 days after
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at DCBOE by 8 p.m. on Election Day, or be postmarked by Election Day and received within 7 days after. May also be dropped in an official drop box.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Washington D.C. has a closed primary (D.C. Code §1-1001.14). Only voters registered with a political party may vote in that party's primary. Unaffiliated and independent voters may not participate in the Democratic or Republican primary.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Washington D.C. tiene una primaria cerrada (D.C. Code §1-1001.14). Solo los votantes registrados en un partido político pueden votar en la primaria de ese partido. Los votantes sin afiliación e independientes no pueden participar en la primaria Demócrata o Republicana.
